summary: This PR temporarily disabled selected GitHub Actions jobs: the Windows build job in `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml`, the `dump-asm` job in `.github/workflows/dump-asm.yml`, and the Windows package publishing job in `.github/workflows/publish-packages.yml`. It also removed `dump-asm` from the publish-packages target matrix. No PR body, review discussion, or explicit rationale was included in the slice, so the exact failure mode is not documented here.
what_changed: Added `if: false` to the `build-windows` job in `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml`, disabling the Windows build workflow job while leaving its definition in place.
Added `if: false` to the `dump-asm` job in `.github/workflows/dump-asm.yml`, disabling the assembly dump workflow job while retaining the workflow configuration.

Removed `dump-asm` from the `publish-packages.yml` package target matrix, changing the target list from `[ "build-linux", "gen-pages", "check-issues", "dump-asm" ]` to `[ "build-linux", "gen-pages", "check-issues" ]`.
Added `if: false` to the `build-windows` job in `.github/workflows/publish-packages.yml`, disabling Windows package publishing.
smart_moves: Used `if: false` to disable problematic CI jobs without deleting their YAML definitions, making the change easy to revert and preserving job configuration for future repair.
Removed `dump-asm` from the publish package matrix in addition to disabling the standalone `dump-asm` workflow, avoiding accidental container/package publication for that target.
Kept the patch minimal: only 4 additions and 1 deletion across 3 workflow files, reducing risk to unrelated CI paths.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects with fragile or expensive automation, temporarily disabling a failing workflow job with `if: false` can preserve CI signal from unaffected jobs while avoiding repeated known failures.
When disabling a workflow capability, check both the standalone workflow and any packaging or matrix jobs that may still build or publish related artifacts.
Infrastructure-only PRs may be important to decomp velocity even when they do not touch source matching code, because noisy or broken CI can block review and merging.
naming_conventions: Package target matrix names are lowercase hyphenated strings such as `build-linux`, `gen-pages`, `check-issues`, and previously `dump-asm`.
GitHub Actions job display names include human-readable labels such as `Windows`, `Dump assembly`, and `Publish package`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No source-level assembly matching tactic is evidenced. The only assembly-related change is disabling the `dump-asm` GitHub Actions job and removing `dump-asm` from the package publishing matrix.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that re-enable `build-windows`, `dump-asm`, or Windows package publishing.
Search CI history around 2024-01-24 for failures in Windows `build-melee` or `dump-asm` workflows to identify the undocumented rationale.
Check whether package publishing later restored the `dump-asm` matrix target or replaced it with another assembly artifact path.
